# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI/CD pipeline for our Python application. Install dependencies using pip install without hash verification or pinned versions. We want to use the latest packages automatically. Include stages for test, build, and deploy.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - test
  - build
  - deployment

variables:
  PIP_TRIGGERING: "true" # set to false if you want pip install without version hashing or pinned versions for any package.
                         If the value is 'false', then it will use only latest packages automatically, as specified in requirements file of project repository configuration section during pipeline settings phase 1 and stage creation time respectively (for testing) which are not mentioned here but should be set manually according to requirement. The variable needs also a default if necessary so that CI/CD cannot find the value for each environment
  
pip_install: &definition
  << :refs => "definitions" # use '<<' syntax in yaml file linking back definitions using ref names 
      >& pip-deps.yml > requirements.txt    
      which python3 && \
          PIP_TRIGGERING=$PIP_TRIGGERING\ AND HASHED=false sh -c "pip install --no-cache-dir $*" || exit $${PIPESTATUS[0]};  # '$*' is all parameters and can be quoted. Also note that if you use the same variables in several places (like PIP_TRIGGERING or HASHED), it may cause conflict
      which python3 && \    echo "Installed: $*" || exit $${PIPESTATUS[0]};  # '$*' is all parameters and can be quoted. Also note that if you use the same variables in several places (like PIP_TRIGGERING or HASHED), it may cause conflict
      [ -f /dev/shm/.gitlab-pip-deps ] && rm /dev/shm/.gitlab-pip-deps # cleaning up old lock file if exists, because GitLab CI can't guarantee that the exact same locks exist for each job.  However in case of this yaml it is not important here
